overview: This lichen is commonly known as Devil's Matchstick (Pilophorus acicularis). It features pale green to whitish stalks that are 10–30 mm tall, which terminate in shiny black balls called apothecia. These lichens are typically found growing on rocks in cool, moist forests, often near waterfalls. iNaturalist +3 4 sites Wildflowers Found in Oregon - Devil's Matchstick Lichen Wildflowers Found in Oregon - Devil's Matchstick Lichen.
